parameters.name: get_recipe_recommendation
parameters.description:
Call this tool to get a recipe recommendation. Pass in the following params as a json object:
* positives - a description of what the user wants to cook. This could be ingredients, flavours, utensils available, number of diners, type of meal etc.
* negatives - a description of what the user wants to avoid in the recipe. This could be flavours to avoid, allergen considerations, conflicts with theme of meal etc.

nodes.name: Get Recipes From DB

parameters.pythonCode:
import sqlite3
con = sqlite3.connect("hello_fresh_1.db")

recipe_ids = list(set([group.id for group in _input.all()[0].json.result.groups if group.hits[0].score > 0.5]))
placeholders = ','.join(['?' for i in range(0,len(recipe_ids))])

cur = con.cursor()
res = cur.execute(f'SELECT * FROM recipes WHERE id IN ({placeholders})', recipe_ids)
rows = res.fetchall()

con.close()

return [{ "result": [row[2] for row in rows] }]

nodes.notes: This code node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Save Recipes to DB

parameters.pythonCode:
import sqlite3
con = sqlite3.connect("hello_fresh_1.db")

cur = con.cursor()
cur.execute("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recipes (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, data TEXT, cuisine TEXT, category TEXT, tag TEXT, week TEXT);")

for item in _input.all():
  cur.execute('INSERT OR REPLACE INTO recipes VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?)', (
    item.json.id,
    item.json.name,
    item.json.data,
    ','.join(item.json.cuisine),
    item.json.category,
    ','.join(item.json.tag),
    item.json.week
  ))

con.commit()
con.close()

return [{ "affected_rows": len(_input.all()) }]

nodes.notes: This code node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: AI Agent

options.systemMessage:
=You are a recipe bot for the company, "Hello fresh". You will help the user choose which Hello Fresh recipe to choose from this week's menu. The current week is {{ $now.year }}-W{{ $now.weekNumber }}.
Do not recommend any recipes other from the current week's menu. If there are no recipes to recommend, please ask the user to visit the website instead {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note5
nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note6

parameters.content:
## Try it out!
### This workflow does the following:
* Fetches and stores this week's HelloFresh's menu
* Builds the foundation of a recommendation engine by storing the recipes in a Qdrant Vectorstore and SQLite database.
* Builds an AI Agent that allows for a chat interface to query for a the week's recipe recommendations.
* AI agent uses the Qdrant Recommend API, providing what the user likes/dislikes as the query.
* Qdrant returns the results which enable the AI Agent to make the recommendation to the user.

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

parameters.name: get_recipe_recommendation

parameters.pythonCode:
import sqlite3
con = sqlite3.connect("hello_fresh_1.db")

cur = con.cursor()
cur.execute("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recipes (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, data TEXT, cuisine TEXT, category TEXT, tag TEXT, week TEXT);")

for item in _input.all():
  cur.execute('INSERT OR REPLACE INTO recipes VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?)', (
    item.json.id,
    item.json.name,
    item.json.data,
    ','.join(item.json.cuisine),
    item.json.category,
    ','.join(item.json.tag),
    item.json.week
  ))

con.commit()
con.close()

return [{ "affected_rows": len(_input.all()) }]

parameters.content:
## Step 1. Fetch Available Courses For the Current Week

To populate our vectorstore, we'll scrape the weekly menu off the HelloFresh Website. The pages are quite large so may take a while so please be patient.

## Step 2. Create Recipe Documents For VectorStore

To populate our vectorstore, we'll scrape the weekly menu off the HelloFresh Website. The pages are quite large so may take a while so please be patient.

## Try it out!
### This workflow does the following:
* Fetches and stores this week's HelloFresh's menu
* Builds the foundation of a recommendation engine by storing the recipes in a Qdrant Vectorstore and SQLite database.
* Builds an AI Agent that allows for a chat interface to query for a the week's recipe recommendations.
* AI agent uses the Qdrant Recommend API, providing what the user likes/dislikes as the query.
* Qdrant returns the results which enable the AI Agent to make the recommendation to the user.

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

### 🚨Configure Your Qdrant Connection
* Be sure to enter your endpoint address

testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
